mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 112.5 signals a strengthening demand for goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.5 million signals a weakening supply of new homes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal income above 11400 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 4.5% signals a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million signals a weakening supply of new homes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.
